questions.question: A binary code that progresses such that only one bit changes between two successive codes is __________.
Why is the Gray code more practical to use when coding the position of a rotating shaft?
The primary use for Gray code is__________.1

answers.a: Coded representation of a shaft's mechanical position.
answers.c: To represent the correct ASCII code to indicate the angular position of a shaft on rotating machinery
answers.d: To convert the angular position of a shaft on rotating machinery into hexadecimal code

questions.question: In a Gray code, each number is 3 greater than the binary representation of that number.
What is the equivalent Binary value of Gray code 100?
